objective: Test whether hard-negative coverage preference learning can keep its coverage-efficiency signal after explicit pass@1, parseability, and reference-distribution constraints.
primary_success_criteria: Constrained DPO is on the coverage/pass@1/forward-token Pareto frontier versus tuned-hot K=4 and sample-more K=8.
Coverage@4 is at least sample-more K=8 coverage or matches it at materially lower forward-token cost.
Pass@1 proxy stays within 2 percentage points of tuned-hot K=4.
Parse successes per task stays within 5 percent of tuned-hot K=4.
Matched shuffled constrained-DPO underperforms real constrained-DPO.
